name: google-crc32c

metadata.name: google-crc32c
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A python wrapper of the C library 'Google CRC32C'

documentation: # `google-crc32c` ![GA](https://img.shields.io/badge/support-GA-gold.svg) [](https://pypi.org/project/google-crc32c) ![Python Versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/google-crc32c) This package wraps the [`google/crc32c`](https://github.com/google/crc32c) hardware-based implementation of the CRC32C hashing algorithm. Multiple wheels are distributed as well as source. If a wheel is not published for the python version and platform you are using, you will need to compile crc32c using a C toolchain. # Currently Published Wheels Wheels are currently published for CPython 3.9, 3.10, 3.11 and 3.12 for multiple architectures. PyPy 3.9 and 3.10 are also supported for Linux. For information on building your own wheels please view [BUILDING.md](BUILDING.md). ## Linux Wheels are published for the following platforms / architectures: - `manylinux2010` platform, `x86_64` and `1686` architectures - `manylinux2014` platform, `aarch64` architecture ### Unsupported Platforms - `manylinux1` platform, `x86_64` architecture support has ended. See https://github.com/pypa/manylinux/issues/994. ## Mac OS Wheels are published for `x86_64` and `arm64` architectures. ## Windows Wheels are published for the `win_amd64` architecture.

classes.name: google_crc32c.cext.Checksum

methods.name: google_crc32c.cext.Checksum.update
documentation:
Update the checksum with a new chunk of data.

Args:
    chunk (Optional[bytes]): a chunk of data used to extend
        the CRC32C checksum.

Hashlib-alike helper for CRC32C operations.

Args:
    initial_value (Optional[bytes]): the initial chunk of data from
        which the CRC32C checksum is computed.  Defaults to b''.

classes.name: google_crc32c._checksum.CommonChecksum

methods.name: google_crc32c._checksum.CommonChecksum.update
documentation:
Update the checksum with a new chunk of data.

Args:
    chunk (Optional[bytes]): a chunk of data used to extend
        the CRC32C checksum.

methods.name: google_crc32c._checksum.CommonChecksum.digest
documentation:
Big-endian order, per RFC 4960.

See: https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/objects#crc32c

Returns:
    bytes: An eight-byte digest string.

methods.name: google_crc32c._checksum.CommonChecksum.hexdigest
documentation:
Like {meth}`digest` except returns as a bytestring of double length.

Returns

: bytes: A sixteen byte digest string, contaiing only hex digits.

methods.name: google_crc32c._checksum.CommonChecksum.copy
documentation:
Create another checksum with the same CRC32C value.

Returns:
    Checksum: the new instance.

methods.name: google_crc32c._checksum.CommonChecksum.consume
documentation:
Consume chunks from a stream, extending our CRC32 checksum.

Args:
    stream (BinaryIO): the stream to consume.
    chunksize (int): the size of the read to perform

Returns:
    Generator[bytes, None, None]: Iterable of the chunks read from the
    stream.

Hashlib-alike helper for CRC32C operations.

This class should not be used directly and requires an update implementation.

Args:
    initial_value (Optional[bytes]): the initial chunk of data from
        which the CRC32C checksum is computed.  Defaults to b''.

functions.name: google_crc32c.python.extend
documentation:
Update an existing CRC checksum with new chunk of data.

Args

: crc (int): An existing CRC check sum.
  chunk (Union\[bytes, List\[int\], Tuple\[int\]\]): A new chunk of data.

  > Intended to be a byte string or similar.

Returns

: int: New CRC checksum computed by extending existing CRC
  with `chunk`.

functions.name: google_crc32c.python.value
documentation:
Compute a CRC checksum for a chunk of data.

Args
: chunk (Union\[bytes, List\[int\], Tuple\[int\]\]): A new chunk of data.

  : Intended to be a byte string or similar.

Returns

: int: New CRC checksum computed for `chunk`.
